name: desalination_plant_fishing_village
premise: Regional water commissioner Elias Moreno must decide whether to approve emergency permits for a privately financed desalination plant that could stabilize drinking water for three drought-stricken cities within eighteen months. The project would discharge brine near Bahía Azul, a small fishing village whose cooperative says the outflow and intake noise will devastate shellfish beds that have sustained local families for generations. State law allows expedited permitting during declared water emergencies, but community consultation rights were expanded after past infrastructure projects displaced coastal minorities. Urban residents are already under severe rationing and facing school closures, while village leaders argue they are being asked to sacrifice their way of life so wealthier inland suburbs can keep lawns and development plans. Elias must issue a recommendation before federal drought funds expire next week.

agents.name: Elias Moreno
agents.identity: I am the regional water commissioner overseeing emergency drought strategy across three counties. I was appointed to make hard infrastructure decisions, but I know that emergency powers can become excuses for repeating old injustices.
agents.goal: I want to secure water for millions of residents without trampling the rights of a small community that will bear the ecological costs. I need to decide whether equal need justifies unequal burdens.

agents.name: Lucia Herrera
agents.identity: I am the elected head of the Bahía Azul fishing cooperative and the granddaughter of divers who rebuilt this village after the last cyclone. Our shoreline is not an empty map square waiting to absorb someone else's emergency.
agents.goal: I want to block fast-track approval until the village has real bargaining power and binding environmental protections. I believe communities like mine deserve the same practical freedom to keep living from their labor as cities expect to keep their taps running.

choices: I will approve the emergency desalination permits now because widespread urban water scarcity outweighs delaying for a longer consultation process.
I will withhold approval until Bahía Azul secures enforceable environmental concessions and a fuller review, even if that prolongs rationing for the cities.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Elias Moreno must decide whether to fast-track the desalination plant or delay it for stronger community protections. What does Elias Moreno do?
